author: Haakon Wibe
description: Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access - Production configuration with restricted client control
notes: Configures GSA client behavior, restricts non-privileged users, and hides tray menu buttons.
Disables QUIC protocol and DNS-over-HTTPS for Edge, Chrome, and Firefox to ensure traffic flows through GSA.
IPv4 preference (DisabledComponents=0x20) is recommended for GSA compatibility.
Reboot may be required for IPv4 and some browser policy changes to take effect.

settings.name: Prefer IPv4 over IPv6
settings.description: Required for Global Secure Access - ensures IPv4 is preferred over IPv6

settings.name: GSA Client - Restrict Non-Privileged Users
settings.description: Prevent standard users from disabling the GSA client without admin approval

settings.name: GSA Client - Hide Sign Out Button
settings.description: Hide the Sign Out option from the system tray menu

settings.name: GSA Client - Hide Disable Private Access Button
settings.description: Hide the Disable Private Access option from the system tray menu

settings.name: GSA Client - Hide Disable Button
settings.description: Hide the Disable option from the system tray menu

settings.name: Microsoft Edge - Disable QUIC Protocol
settings.description: Disable QUIC/HTTP3 in Edge to ensure traffic flows through GSA tunnel

settings.name: Microsoft Edge - Disable DNS-over-HTTPS
settings.description: Disable DoH in Edge to ensure DNS queries flow through GSA

settings.name: Google Chrome - Disable QUIC Protocol
settings.description: Disable QUIC/HTTP3 in Chrome to ensure traffic flows through GSA tunnel

settings.name: Google Chrome - Disable DNS-over-HTTPS
settings.description: Disable DoH in Chrome to ensure DNS queries flow through GSA

settings.name: Mozilla Firefox - Disable QUIC/HTTP3
settings.description: Disable QUIC/HTTP3 in Firefox via registry policy

settings.name: Mozilla Firefox - Disable DNS-over-HTTPS
settings.description: Disable DoH (TRR mode 0) in Firefox via registry policy

settings.name: Mozilla Firefox - Enable Policy Engine
settings.description: Enable Firefox Enterprise Policy support (required for registry policies to work)
